highlights.text: I wrote at the close Monday that: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
My lean was higher, stating yesterday at 4pm: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
Bear case begins below 6752, after a trap.” Today we saw exactly this - we recovered 6765 last evening, then ran to 6795 by this morning.
One of the cycles for example is that institutions buy dips using my core setup (the Failed Breakdown), which then creates a short squeeze.
Failed Breakdowns are always preceded by “elevator down” sells.
This cycle which I call “elevator down-into-short squeeze” is extremely common, and its what gave us the rally from last Tuesday’s low at 6680, into last weeks high of 6800.

I wrote last Tuesday at 4pm: “Another option to long is the Failed Breakdown of today’s daily low at 6693.” Early Wednesday morning we flushed 6693 down to 6680, recovered 6693, then went parabolic to 6800 high last week.
Sideways ranges see ample Failed Breakdowns.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into a quality Failed Breakdown we had late day Friday which ultimately propelled the terminal leg into this weeks high (this one was very textbook and good for learning).
ES is rangebound in a big range 6750 to 6800, with 6765 being a big magnet/pivot in the middle.

post_title: Bulls Bought The SPX Mini-Dip Again. Parabolic Leg Up Coming, Or Will They Drop The Ball? Oct 9 Plan
